preamble: The diphthong /au/, commonly found in Latin and Proto-Indo-European, has undergone various transformations across its descendant languages. One notable, though less widespread, historical phonetic shift is its evolution to the diphthong /oi/. This change involves a complex interplay of vowel modifications, resulting in a diphthong with a more fronted and often higher second element.
phoneticEffects: This transformation typically involves the raising and rounding of the initial vowel element of the diphthong, from an open back unrounded or open-mid back rounded vowel to a mid-back rounded vowel

examples.note: Latin 'aurum' (gold) evolved to 'oiru' in Fala, demonstrating the /au/ to /oi/ shift.
